title: Cloud Strategy & Governance
verbatim: Financial institutions shall establish a cloud strategy and governance framework that defines the institution's approach to cloud adoption, approved cloud service models, and governance structures for cloud-related decisions. The strategy shall be aligned with the institution's overall technology strategy and risk appetite.
translation: Develop a board-approved cloud strategy that defines your approach to cloud adoption, which service models are approved, and how cloud decisions are governed. Align it with your overall technology strategy and risk appetite.
evidence: Approved cloud service models and providers

artifacts.name: Cloud Strategy
artifacts.description: Board-approved cloud strategy defining approach to cloud adoption

artifacts.name: Cloud Governance Framework
artifacts.description: Framework defining governance structures for cloud-related decisions

artifacts.name: Strategy Alignment Documentation
artifacts.description: Documentation showing alignment between cloud and overall technology strategy

title: Cloud Security Controls
verbatim: Financial institutions shall implement security controls for cloud environments that address identity and access management, data protection, network security, logging and monitoring, and configuration management. Controls shall be aligned with the shared responsibility model and validated through regular assessments.
translation: Implement cloud-specific security controls for IAM, data protection, network security, logging, and configuration management. These controls must align with the shared responsibility model and be regularly tested to ensure they work.

artifacts.name: Cloud Security Control Framework
artifacts.description: Framework defining security controls for cloud environments

artifacts.name: Cloud Security Configuration
artifacts.description: Configuration evidence for cloud IAM, data protection, and network security

artifacts.name: Cloud Security Assessment Report
artifacts.description: Regular assessment reports validating cloud security control effectiveness

title: Cloud Portability & Exit
verbatim: Financial institutions shall ensure that cloud architectures support portability and avoid excessive dependency on a single cloud service provider. Exit strategies shall be maintained for all cloud services and shall include data extraction procedures, transition timelines, and contingency arrangements.
translation: Design cloud architectures for portability to avoid vendor lock-in. Maintain exit strategies for every cloud service covering data extraction, transition timelines, and contingency plans in case you need to move away from a provider.

artifacts.name: Cloud Exit Strategy
artifacts.description: Exit strategies for all cloud services with data extraction and transition plans

artifacts.name: Cloud Portability Architecture
artifacts.description: Architecture documentation demonstrating cloud portability and reduced vendor lock-in

artifacts.name: Vendor Lock-In Risk Assessment
artifacts.description: Risk assessment of vendor lock-in for each cloud service

title: AI & Machine Learning Governance
verbatim: Financial institutions should establish governance frameworks for the use of artificial intelligence and machine learning in decision-making processes. The framework should address model risk management, data quality, bias detection, explainability, and accountability. AI models used for customer-facing decisions should be subject to regular validation.
translation: If you use AI or ML for decision-making, establish a governance framework covering model risk, data quality, bias, explainability, and accountability. AI models that affect customers should be regularly validated to ensure fairness and accuracy.

artifacts.name: AI/ML Governance Framework
artifacts.description: Framework governing AI and ML use including model risk, bias, and explainability

artifacts.name: Model Risk Management Procedures
artifacts.description: Procedures for managing risks associated with AI and ML models

artifacts.name: AI Model Validation Reports
artifacts.description: Validation reports for AI models used in customer-facing decisions

title: Emerging Technology Risk Assessment
verbatim: Financial institutions should conduct risk assessments for the adoption of emerging technologies including distributed ledger technology, quantum computing preparedness, and Internet of Things deployments. Risk assessments should consider security, privacy, regulatory, and operational implications prior to adoption.
translation: Before adopting emerging technologies like blockchain, IoT, or preparing for quantum computing impacts, conduct risk assessments covering security, privacy, regulatory, and operational implications. Understand the risks before committing.
evidence: Completed risk assessments for adopted emerging technologies

artifacts.name: Emerging Technology Risk Assessment Framework
artifacts.description: Framework for assessing risks of emerging technology adoption

artifacts.name: Emerging Technology Risk Assessments
artifacts.description: Risk assessment reports for emerging technologies under consideration or adopted

artifacts.name: Quantum Readiness Assessment
artifacts.description: Assessment of preparedness for quantum computing impacts on cryptography
